Breeders left with an ineligible foal have been given a respite with Racing Australia announcing an amnesty for 2024 and 2025 born foals. All previously ineligible foals are now eligible to race, even if the appeal was declined, and those who haven’t done their FODs have until April 14, 2026 to ensure their foal can race. (Racing Australia Offers Amnesty for Ineligible Foals)

The catalogue for the 2026 Inglis Australian Weanling Sale has been released. The final crop of Snitzel is represented, as well as opportunities to purchase the offspring of first-season sires such as Shinzo and Ozzmosis in a big book of 500 lots. The sale will take place on May 5 and 6 at the Riverside Stables complex, ahead of the Inglis Chairman’s Sale on Thursday, May 7. (Inglis Australian Weanling Sale Catalogue Released, Featuring Snitzel's Final Crop)

As a key sale on the Australian calendar, the Inglis Easter Yearling Sale results demonstrate the health of the top end of the market. Held in 2026 under a difficult economic climate, the lower clearance rate led to a lower gross, but the popular lots sold well pushing up the average. (Inglis Easter Yearling Sale: Mixed Results Reflect Top-End Market Health)

The 2026 Inglis Australian Easter Yearling Sale concluded on Monday with another day of dazzling results, hugely successful pinhooks, and a noticeable dominance by super sire Extreme Choice, who averaged a record-breaking $1.56 million across the sale. (Extreme Choice Dominates 2026 Inglis Easter Yearling Sale, Averages Record $1.56M)

Day 2 of the 2026 Inglis Australian Easter Yearling Sale delivered a wave of elite results, with 18 yearlings selling for $1 million or more, lifting the overall tally to 26 seven-figure lots across the sale. (Inglis Easter Day 2 Delivers Elite Results: 26 Seven-Figure Lots)

While the bidding warmed up at the Riverside Stables sales complex on Monday morning, just over the fence at Warwick Farm was where the trial action was located. Private Harry stepped out for Chris Waller for the first time, while smart fillies by Wootton Bassett and Snitzel had sharp hit-outs in the juvenile heats. (Warwick Farm Trials See Private Harry Debut, Impressive Juvenile Fillies)

Mark Newnham took back the top spot in the trainers' premiership with a treble at Sha Tin on Sunday night, while Frankie Lor's super 3-year-old Salon S posted his fourth straight win since landing in the jurisdiction to remain unbeaten. (Newnham Retakes Trainers' Lead with Sha Tin Treble; Salon S Extends Win Streak)

The first day selling at the the 2026 Inglis Australian Easter Yearling Sale has drawn to a close, and 149 yearlings have found new homes. Across the day, there were multi-million dollar highs, extensive shopping lists, and future aspirations being traded. (Inglis Easter Day 1: 149 Yearlings Sold Amid Multi-Million Dollar Highs)
